title: 2032 World Height
description: Increase your build limit to Y=2031 in all dimensions.
body:
# **Increase the build limit to Y=2031 in all dimensions.** ## **WARNING**: Starting with Minecraft 1.21.2 removing the datapack/mod will corrupt your world! You can find a tutorial on how to remove it safely [here](https://www.reddit.com/r/feedthebeast/comments/1m7qcy6/how_to_recover_world_from_chunk_height_corruption/). ### Does this support custom world generation mods/datapacks? This datapack doesn't change world generation by itself, but custom world generation mods/datapacks may hardcode the world height. A notable example is Tectonic. The mod version of it is not supported, but if you're using the datapack version you go to /data/minecraft/dimension_type/overworld.json inside the Tectonic datapack folder and change "logical_height" and "height" to 2096. ### Does this work with existing worlds? Officially no, but you can follow [this tutorial](https://www.reddit.com/r/feedthebeast/s/2uv5jOTJTW) to transfer existing chunks to a world made with this datapack. ### What happens to existing worlds if I remove the mod/datapack? Removing the datapack/mod in 1.17-1.21.1 will make everything above normal build limit disappear, but since 1.21.2 it will corrupt the world, thus the warning at the top.
*If you still don't have a server host I recommend BisectHosting. They have 20 server locations, advanced DDoS protection, an user-friendly panel for server management and if you use promo code OBYDUX you get 25% off.*
